node.title: Codify runtime generations, consistency envelopes, and hard budgets

acceptance.label: Architecture contract names the runtime generations, freshness tiers, correctness envelope, memory budgets, and no-compromise latency targets.

node.title: Build WorkspaceRuntimeEngine and the coalescing-aware command model

acceptance.label: A workspace engine exists with a command queue API that natively models coalescing, cancellation, result delivery, and serialized authoritative commits.

node.title: Separate mutable engine state from published immutable generations

acceptance.label: Queries read published generations, working mutations no longer rebuild from published Prism state, and retained generation count and memory shape are explicitly bounded.

node.title: Remove reconstructive refresh and ordinary live-state indexer rebuilds

acceptance.label: Routine refreshes no longer call the reconstructive live-state indexer path, and ordinary edit handling mutates long-lived engine state incrementally.

node.title: Implement persistent per-file semantic facts and delta batches

acceptance.label: The engine persists file-local semantic facts explicitly and accepts merged delta batches instead of reconstructive refresh plans for ordinary edits.

node.title: Add precise invalidation and reverse dependency indexes

acceptance.label: The engine can derive precise affected sets from a changed file or delta batch without broad workspace-wide invalidation heuristics.

node.title: Parallelize file-local preparation for bursty batched edits

acceptance.label: Many-file edit bursts are coalesced and prepared in parallel, and stale intermediate file versions are cancelled instead of fully processed.

node.title: Implement the fast-path commit and layered freshness semantics

acceptance.label: Small edits publish a new generation after a short serialized commit, and all unsettled downstream domains are surfaced explicitly through freshness/materialization metadata.

node.title: Add parallel settle workers, prioritization, and stale-work cancellation

acceptance.label: Downstream recomputation runs in parallel outside the commit path, direct dependents are prioritized, and stale settle work is cancelled when superseded by newer generations.

node.title: Move non-authoritative side effects off commit and demote snapshots

acceptance.label: Routine commits no longer wait on memory reanchor or snapshot-shaped persistence, and snapshots are no longer the authoritative mutation substrate anywhere in the ordinary edit path.

node.title: Replace lock-based fail-fast admission with engine-queued mutation admission

acceptance.label: Ordinary mutation overlap is handled by engine admission and bounded waiting, and the old fail-fast refresh_lock busy semantics are no longer the normal user-facing experience.

node.title: Finalize checkpoint-plus-replay recovery on the incremental core

acceptance.label: Startup hydrates from checkpoints plus replay against the incremental engine, with bounded replay cost and no reintroduction of snapshot-as-authority semantics.

node.title: Validate the performance-beast end state and remove backsliding surfaces

acceptance.label: The new runtime meets the agreed interactivity and memory budgets in automated and live validation, and the old reconstructive snapshot surfaces are retired or clearly demoted.

node.title: Codify runtime generations, consistency envelopes, and hard budgets
node.summary: Define the canonical runtime state machine up front: working generation, published generation, settle frontier, checkpoint generation, dirty domains, freshness tiers, query consistency contracts, memory budgets, and latency SLOs. Lock in the rule that the fast path may be conservative but must never be silently misleading, and encode the parallel-prepare / serialized-commit / parallel-settle model as architecture, not implementation detail.

acceptance.label: Architecture contract names the runtime generations, freshness tiers, correctness envelope, memory budgets, and no-compromise latency targets.

node.title: Build WorkspaceRuntimeEngine and the coalescing-aware command model
node.summary: Introduce a single-writer WorkspaceRuntimeEngine per workspace that owns authoritative mutable semantic state and accepts coalescing-aware commands. The actor must own state transitions and commit ordering, while allowing heavy parse and analysis work to happen off-actor against immutable inputs and return deterministic deltas.

acceptance.label: A workspace engine exists with a command queue API that natively models coalescing, cancellation, result delivery, and serialized authoritative commits.

node.title: Separate mutable engine state from published immutable generations
node.summary: Make published Prism generations an immutable read surface instead of the mutable working substrate. The engine should retain long-lived mutable state internally, publish structurally shared immutable generations for readers, retain only a bounded generation window, and attach explicit freshness/materialization metadata to every published generation.

acceptance.label: Queries read published generations, working mutations no longer rebuild from published Prism state, and retained generation count and memory shape are explicitly bounded.

node.title: Remove reconstructive refresh and ordinary live-state indexer rebuilds
node.summary: test summary payload shape

acceptance.label: Routine refreshes no longer call the reconstructive live-state indexer path, and ordinary edit handling mutates long-lived engine state incrementally.

node.title: Codify runtime generations, consistency envelopes, and hard budgets
node.summary: Define the canonical runtime state machine up front: working generation, published generation, settle frontier, checkpoint generation, dirty domains, freshness tiers, query consistency contracts, memory budgets, and latency SLOs. Lock in the rule that the fast path may be conservative but must never be silently misleading, and encode the parallel-prepare / serialized-commit / parallel-settle model as architecture, not implementation detail.

acceptance.label: Architecture contract names the runtime generations, freshness tiers, correctness envelope, memory budgets, and no-compromise latency targets.

node.title: Build WorkspaceRuntimeEngine and the coalescing-aware command model
node.summary: Introduce a single-writer WorkspaceRuntimeEngine per workspace that owns authoritative mutable semantic state and accepts coalescing-aware commands. The actor must own state transitions and commit ordering, while allowing heavy parse and analysis work to happen off-actor against immutable inputs and return deterministic deltas.

acceptance.label: A workspace engine exists with a command queue API that natively models coalescing, cancellation, result delivery, and serialized authoritative commits.

node.title: Separate mutable engine state from published immutable generations
node.summary: Make published Prism generations an immutable read surface instead of the mutable working substrate. The engine should retain long-lived mutable state internally, publish structurally shared immutable generations for readers, retain only a bounded generation window, and attach explicit freshness/materialization metadata to every published generation.

acceptance.label: Queries read published generations, working mutations no longer rebuild from published Prism state, and retained generation count and memory shape are explicitly bounded.

node.title: Remove reconstructive refresh and ordinary live-state indexer rebuilds
node.summary: Remove the ordinary edit-time path that rebuilds a fresh working indexer from published Prism state. Routine refreshes must mutate long-lived engine state incrementally, so reconstructive refresh remains only as an exceptional fallback and not as the steady-state substrate.

acceptance.label: Routine refreshes no longer call the reconstructive live-state indexer path, and ordinary edit handling mutates long-lived engine state incrementally.

node.title: Implement persistent per-file semantic facts and delta batches
node.summary: Introduce explicit per-file semantic facts as the core mutable data model: source hash, parsed/local declarations, imports, unresolved references, local graph fragments, and lineage links. Ordinary edits should enter the engine as merged delta batches over those facts instead of snapshot-shaped refresh plans.

acceptance.label: The engine persists file-local semantic facts explicitly and accepts merged delta batches instead of reconstructive refresh plans for ordinary edits.

node.title: Add precise invalidation and reverse dependency indexes
node.summary: Build precise invalidation and reverse dependency indexes over files, symbols, packages, and downstream consumers. A changed file or delta batch should produce a narrow affected-set frontier, not broad workspace-wide resolution walks or heuristic rescans.

acceptance.label: The engine can derive precise affected sets from a changed file or delta batch without broad workspace-wide invalidation heuristics.

node.title: Parallelize file-local preparation for bursty batched edits
node.summary: Make coalescing a native part of the command model and parallelize file-local preparation aggressively. Bursty editor or agent writes must collapse obsolete intermediate versions, parallelize file-local read/parse/extract work, and merge only the newest surviving deltas into the engine commit batch.

acceptance.label: Many-file edit bursts are coalesced and prepared in parallel, and stale intermediate file versions are cancelled instead of fully processed.

node.title: Implement the fast-path commit and layered freshness semantics
node.summary: Define and implement the fast-path correctness envelope: small edits publish a new generation after a minimal serialized commit, while unsettled downstream domains are surfaced explicitly through layered freshness metadata. The fast path may be conservative, but it must never be silently misleading about unsettled cross-file facts.

acceptance.label: Small edits publish a new generation after a short serialized commit, and all unsettled downstream domains are surfaced explicitly through freshness/materialization metadata.

node.title: Add parallel settle workers, prioritization, and stale-work cancellation
node.summary: Run downstream recomputation in parallel settle workers outside the commit path. Prioritize the changed file, direct dependents, and likely query targets first; support stale-work cancellation when newer generations supersede older settle tasks; and expose queue depth and settle-lag telemetry as first-class runtime metrics.

acceptance.label: Downstream recomputation runs in parallel outside the commit path, direct dependents are prioritized, and stale settle work is cancelled when superseded by newer generations.

node.title: Move non-authoritative side effects off commit and demote snapshots
node.summary: Move non-authoritative side effects fully behind the fast commit and demote snapshots to their final roles only: immutable read views, query acceleration, and checkpoint recovery. Memory reanchor, projections, curator follow-up, checkpoints, and other secondary work must no longer hold up the authoritative runtime commit.

acceptance.label: Routine commits no longer wait on memory reanchor or snapshot-shaped persistence, and snapshots are no longer the authoritative mutation substrate anywhere in the ordinary edit path.

node.title: Replace lock-based fail-fast admission with engine-queued mutation admission
node.summary: Replace the current layered fail-fast lock UX with engine-owned queued or bounded-wait admission. The authoritative engine should serialize commits, but normal user-facing mutations should no longer surface raw refresh_lock or sync_lock contention as the primary interaction model.

acceptance.label: Ordinary mutation overlap is handled by engine admission and bounded waiting, and the old fail-fast refresh_lock busy semantics are no longer the normal user-facing experience.

node.title: Finalize checkpoint-plus-replay recovery on the incremental core
node.summary: Rebuild startup and crash recovery around checkpoint generations plus incremental replay on top of the new semantic database. Recovery must preserve the incremental authority model, keep replay costs bounded, and avoid reintroducing snapshot-as-authority semantics through the back door.

acceptance.label: Startup hydrates from checkpoints plus replay against the incremental engine, with bounded replay cost and no reintroduction of snapshot-as-authority semantics.

node.title: Validate the performance-beast end state and remove backsliding surfaces
node.summary: Validate the full performance-beast end state against hard budgets: tiny-edit fast-path latency, many-file burst throughput, queue wait time, settle lag, restart recovery time, and bounded memory. Retire or clearly demote old reconstructive snapshot surfaces so the old architecture cannot drift back in unnoticed.

acceptance.label: The new runtime meets the agreed interactivity and memory budgets in automated and live validation, and the old reconstructive snapshot surfaces are retired or clearly demoted.

node.title: Codify runtime generations, consistency envelopes, and hard budgets
node.summary: Define the canonical runtime state machine up front for a bound worktree context: working generation, published generation, settle frontier, checkpoint generation, dirty domains, domain-scoped freshness tiers, query consistency contracts, memory budgets, and latency SLOs. Make the monotonic committed delta journal a first-class part of the architecture alongside checkpoint-plus-replay recovery, and lock in the rule that the fast path may be conservative but must never be silently misleading. The architecture contract should explicitly encode the parallel-prepare / serialized-commit / parallel-settle model as a core semantic boundary, not an implementation detail.

acceptance.label: Architecture contract names the runtime generations, domain-scoped freshness tiers, committed delta journal, correctness envelope, memory budgets, and no-compromise latency targets.

node.title: Build WorkspaceRuntimeEngine and the coalescing-aware command model
node.summary: Introduce a single-writer WorkspaceRuntimeEngine per bound worktree context that owns authoritative mutable semantic state and accepts coalescing-aware commands. The actor is the commit kernel: it owns admission, authoritative state transitions, commit ordering, generation publication, and queue-class policy, while heavy parse and analysis work stays off-actor against immutable inputs and returns deterministic deltas for commit. The queue model should support priority classes, coalescing, cancellation, and bounded interactivity under bursty editor and agent traffic.

acceptance.label: A worktree-scoped workspace engine exists with a command queue API that models priority classes, coalescing, cancellation, result delivery, and serialized authoritative commits without turning the actor into the general compute sink.

node.title: Separate mutable engine state from published immutable generations
node.summary: Make published Prism generations an immutable read surface instead of the mutable working substrate. The engine should retain long-lived mutable state internally, publish structurally shared immutable generations for readers, retain only a bounded generation window, and attach explicit per-domain freshness and materialization state to every published generation so readers can distinguish file-local currentness, unsettled cross-file facts, pending reanchor work, and older but valid checkpoints.

acceptance.label: Queries read published generations whose retained memory shape is explicitly bounded and whose per-domain freshness/materialization state is visible rather than implied.

node.title: Implement the fast-path commit and layered freshness semantics
node.summary: Define and implement the fast-path correctness envelope: small edits publish a new generation after a minimal serialized commit, emit a monotonic committed delta batch suitable for replay and downstream consumers, and surface unsettled downstream domains explicitly through layered freshness metadata. The fast path may be conservative, but it must never be silently misleading about unsettled cross-file facts or stale derived domains.

acceptance.label: Small edits publish a new generation after a short serialized commit, emit a replayable committed delta batch, and surface all unsettled downstream domains explicitly through freshness/materialization metadata.

node.title: Validate the performance-beast end state and remove backsliding surfaces
node.summary: Validate the full performance-beast end state against hard budgets: tiny-edit fast-path latency, many-file burst throughput, queue wait time, settle lag, restart recovery time, and bounded memory. Validate generation drift behavior on handles, tool outputs, and published read surfaces; validate that unsettled domains are surfaced rather than silently hidden; and retire or clearly demote old reconstructive snapshot surfaces so the old architecture cannot drift back in unnoticed.

acceptance.label: The new runtime meets the agreed interactivity and memory budgets in automated and live validation, and generation drift plus unsettled-domain visibility are explicitly tested across handles, tool outputs, and published read surfaces.

node.title: Preserve the three-plane persistence split and the machine-scoped shared runtime host

acceptance.label: The rewrite preserves repo-published truth, a machine-local shared runtime store, and worktree-scoped runtime engines under one machine-scoped MCP host, with a backend-neutral shared-runtime store seam that can later take Postgres without another architecture rewrite.

node.title: Preserve the three-plane persistence split and the machine-scoped shared runtime host
node.summary: Preserve PRISM's three-plane persistence split as a hard architectural boundary: repo-published truth stays in `.prism`, shared mutable runtime state lives in one machine-local shared runtime database, and each active workspace or worktree gets its own authoritative WorkspaceRuntimeEngine inside one machine-scoped MCP host. The rewrite must keep the shared-runtime store backend-neutral so SQLite remains the local implementation now while Postgres can later replace it to extend the same coordination model across machines without another semantic rewrite.

acceptance.label: The rewrite preserves repo truth, a single machine-local shared runtime store, and worktree-scoped engines under one machine host, with a backend-neutral shared-runtime store seam that can later take Postgres without another architecture rewrite.

node.title: Codify runtime generations, consistency envelopes, and hard budgets
node.summary: Define the canonical runtime state machine up front for a bound worktree context inside a machine-scoped MCP host: working generation, published generation, settle frontier, checkpoint generation, dirty domains, domain-scoped freshness tiers, query consistency contracts, memory budgets, and latency SLOs. Make the monotonic committed delta journal a first-class part of the architecture alongside checkpoint-plus-replay recovery, preserve the three-plane persistence split explicitly, and lock in the rule that the fast path may be conservative but must never be silently misleading. The architecture contract should explicitly encode the parallel-prepare / serialized-commit / parallel-settle model as a core semantic boundary, not an implementation detail.

acceptance.label: Architecture contract names the runtime generations, domain-scoped freshness tiers, committed delta journal, three-plane persistence boundaries, correctness envelope, memory budgets, and no-compromise latency targets.

node.title: Build WorkspaceRuntimeEngine and the coalescing-aware command model
node.summary: Introduce a single-writer WorkspaceRuntimeEngine per bound worktree context inside one machine-scoped MCP host that owns authoritative mutable semantic state and accepts coalescing-aware commands. The actor is the commit kernel: it owns admission, authoritative state transitions, commit ordering, generation publication, and queue-class policy, while heavy parse and analysis work stays off-actor against immutable inputs and returns deterministic deltas for commit. The host must be able to run many worktree-scoped engines concurrently against one machine-local shared runtime store without blurring per-worktree authority.

acceptance.label: A worktree-scoped workspace engine exists under a machine-scoped host with a queue API that models priority classes, coalescing, cancellation, and serialized authoritative commits while allowing multiple engines to share one backend runtime store safely.

node.title: Finalize checkpoint-plus-replay recovery on the incremental core
node.summary: Rebuild startup and crash recovery around checkpoint generations plus incremental replay on top of the new semantic database, while preserving the machine-local shared runtime store as the durability layer for shared mutable state and keeping the store boundary backend-neutral. Recovery must preserve the incremental authority model, keep replay costs bounded, support one machine-scoped host serving multiple worktree engines, and avoid reintroducing snapshot-as-authority semantics through the back door.

acceptance.label: Startup and crash recovery use checkpoint-plus-replay over the incremental core while preserving a backend-neutral shared-runtime store contract that works for one machine-local SQLite store now and can later take Postgres without changing runtime semantics.

node.title: Validate the performance-beast end state and remove backsliding surfaces
node.summary: Validate the full performance-beast end state against hard budgets: tiny-edit fast-path latency, many-file burst throughput, queue wait time, settle lag, restart recovery time, and bounded memory. Validate generation drift behavior on handles, tool outputs, and published read surfaces; validate that unsettled domains are surfaced rather than silently hidden; and validate the machine-scoped host model with multiple worktree-scoped engines sharing one local runtime store for the same repo. Retire or clearly demote old reconstructive snapshot surfaces so the old architecture cannot drift back in unnoticed.

acceptance.label: The new runtime meets the agreed interactivity and memory budgets, surfaces unsettled domains explicitly, and proves that multiple worktree-scoped engines can safely share one machine-local runtime store without violating the three-plane persistence split.

The architecture contract should explicitly encode the parallel-prepare / serialized-commit / parallel-settle model as a core semantic boundary, not an implementation detail.

acceptance.label: Architecture contract names the runtime generations, domain-scoped freshness tiers, committed delta journal, three-plane persistence boundaries, correctness envelope, memory budgets, and no-compromise latency targets.

node.summary: Preserve PRISM's three-plane persistence split as a hard architectural boundary: repo-published truth stays in `.prism`, shared mutable runtime state lives in one machine-local shared runtime database, and each active workspace or worktree gets its own authoritative WorkspaceRuntimeEngine inside one machine-scoped MCP host. The rewrite must keep the shared-runtime store backend-neutral so SQLite remains the local implementation now while Postgres can later replace it to extend the same coordination model across machines without another semantic rewrite.

acceptance.label: The rewrite preserves repo truth, a single machine-local shared runtime store, and worktree-scoped engines under one machine host, with a backend-neutral shared-runtime store seam that can later take Postgres without another architecture rewrite.

node.title: Build WorkspaceRuntimeEngine and the coalescing-aware command model
node.summary: Introduce a single-writer WorkspaceRuntimeEngine per bound worktree context inside one machine-scoped MCP host that owns authoritative mutable semantic state and accepts coalescing-aware commands. The actor is the commit kernel: it owns admission, authoritative state transitions, commit ordering, generation publication, and queue-class policy, while heavy parse and analysis work stays off-actor against immutable inputs and returns deterministic deltas for commit. The host must be able to run many worktree-scoped engines concurrently against one machine-local shared runtime store without blurring per-worktree authority.

acceptance.label: A worktree-scoped workspace engine exists under a machine-scoped host with a queue API that models priority classes, coalescing, cancellation, and serialized authoritative commits while allowing multiple engines to share one backend runtime store safely.

node.title: Build WorkspaceRuntimeEngine and the coalescing-aware command model
node.summary: Introduce a single-writer WorkspaceRuntimeEngine per bound worktree context inside one machine-scoped MCP host that owns authoritative mutable semantic state and accepts coalescing-aware commands. The actor is the commit kernel: it owns admission, authoritative state transitions, commit ordering, generation publication, and queue-class policy, while heavy parse and analysis work stays off-actor against immutable inputs and returns deterministic deltas for commit. One daemon per machine is the default deployment shape, but the host and backend interfaces must remain multi-instance-clean so multiple daemons can safely share one machine-local runtime store without blurring per-worktree authority.

acceptance.label: A worktree-scoped workspace engine exists under the default machine-scoped host shape with queue classes, coalescing, cancellation, and serialized authoritative commits, while preserving correctness if multiple local MCP daemons share the same backend runtime store.

node.title: Finalize checkpoint-plus-replay recovery on the incremental core
node.summary: Rebuild startup and crash recovery around checkpoint generations plus incremental replay on top of the new semantic database, while preserving the machine-local shared runtime store as the durability layer for shared mutable state and keeping the store boundary backend-neutral. Recovery and continuity semantics must remain multi-instance-clean even in the local SQLite phase: use explicit instance identity, leases or heartbeats where needed, and revision-aware writes so one daemon per machine is merely the default deployment shape rather than a hidden correctness assumption.

acceptance.label: Startup and crash recovery use checkpoint-plus-replay over the incremental core while preserving a backend-neutral shared-runtime store contract that remains correct under multiple local MCP daemons sharing one runtime store and can later take Postgres without changing runtime semantics.

node.title: Finalize checkpoint-plus-replay recovery on the incremental core
node.summary: Rebuild startup and crash recovery around checkpoint generations plus incremental replay on top of the new semantic database, while preserving the machine-local shared runtime store as the durability layer for shared mutable state and keeping the store boundary backend-neutral. Recovery must preserve the incremental authority model, keep replay costs bounded, support one machine-scoped host serving multiple worktree engines, and avoid reintroducing snapshot-as-authority semantics through the back door.

acceptance.label: Startup and crash recovery use checkpoint-plus-replay over the incremental core while preserving a backend-neutral shared-runtime store contract that remains correct under multiple local MCP daemons sharing one runtime store and can later take Postgres without changing runtime semantics.

node.title: Validate the performance-beast end state and remove backsliding surfaces
node.summary: Validate the full performance-beast end state against hard budgets: tiny-edit fast-path latency, many-file burst throughput, queue wait time, settle lag, restart recovery time, and bounded memory. Validate generation drift behavior on handles, tool outputs, and published read surfaces; validate that unsettled domains are surfaced rather than silently hidden; validate the default machine-scoped host model with multiple worktree-scoped engines sharing one local runtime store for the same repo; and validate that multiple MCP daemons on one machine can also share that store safely without violating the three-plane persistence split.

acceptance.label: The new runtime meets the agreed interactivity and memory budgets, surfaces unsettled domains explicitly, supports the default one-daemon-per-machine topology, and remains correct when multiple local MCP daemons share one runtime store for the same repo without violating the three-plane persistence split.

acceptance.label: The new runtime meets the agreed interactivity and memory budgets, surfaces unsettled domains explicitly, supports the default one-daemon-per-machine topology, and remains correct when multiple local MCP daemons share one runtime store for the same repo without violating the three-plane persistence split.

plan.goal: incremental-semantic-db-runtime-engine-rewrite: transform PRISM into a true incremental semantic database with a WorkspaceRuntimeEngine actor, heavy parallel preparation and settle pipelines, coalesced edit batches, a minimal serialized commit, and snapshots limited to checkpoint recovery and query acceleration.

node.title: Build an incremental diagnostics and runtime read-model plane

acceptance.label: The host owns bounded materialized read models for runtime status, dashboard summary, and recent operation state that are fed incrementally from engine commits, process lifecycle events, and operation events instead of being rebuilt on request.

plan.title: incremental-semantic-db-runtime-engine-rewrite: transform PRISM into a true incremental semantic database with a WorkspaceRuntimeEngine actor, heavy parallel preparation and settle pipelines, coalesced edit batches, a minimal serialized commit, and snapshots limited to checkpoint recovery and query acceleration.

node.title: Implement a cheap machine-scoped process and instance ledger for diagnostics

acceptance.label: Runtime process and daemon/bridge status comes from an instance-aware process ledger with bounded liveness checks, not broad request-time ps scans, while staying correct for one or many local MCP daemons sharing the same runtime store.

node.title: Route runtimeStatus, dashboard summary, and recent operation views to materialized read models

acceptance.label: Common diagnostics surfaces no longer perform request-time workspace walks, plan graph reconstruction, query-log file reparsing, or broad process scans; they read bounded materialized views that expose explicit domain-scoped freshness and lag state.

node.title: Validate the performance-beast end state and remove backsliding surfaces
node.summary: Validate the full performance-beast end state against hard budgets: tiny-edit fast-path latency, many-file burst throughput, queue wait time, settle lag, restart recovery time, bounded memory, and bounded diagnostics latency. Validate generation drift behavior on handles, tool outputs, and published read surfaces; validate that unsettled domains are surfaced rather than silently hidden; validate the machine-scoped host model with multiple worktree-scoped engines sharing one local runtime store for the same repo; and validate that runtimeStatus, dashboard summary, and recent operation surfaces read bounded materialized diagnostics views rather than reconstructing state on request. Retire or clearly demote old reconstructive snapshot and diagnostics surfaces so the old architecture cannot drift back in unnoticed.

acceptance.label: The new runtime meets the agreed interactivity, diagnostics-latency, and memory budgets, surfaces unsettled domains explicitly, and keeps runtime status and dashboard surfaces on bounded materialized read models instead of request-time reconstruction.

node.title: Separate mutable engine state from published immutable generations
node.summary: Make published immutable generations the read-facing artifact of the engine and carry enough structured metadata for both semantic queries and bounded diagnostics. Queries and diagnostics read published generations or materialized read-model derivatives from them; working mutations no longer rebuild from published Prism state, and retained generation count and memory shape remain explicitly bounded.

acceptance.label: Queries and diagnostics read published generations or bounded materialized derivatives, working mutations no longer rebuild from published Prism state, and retained generation count and memory shape are explicitly bounded.

node.title: Validate the performance-beast end state and remove backsliding surfaces
node.summary: Validate the full performance-beast end state against hard budgets: tiny-edit fast-path latency, many-file burst throughput, queue wait time, settle lag, restart recovery time, and bounded memory. Validate generation drift behavior on handles, tool outputs, and published read surfaces; validate that unsettled domains are surfaced rather than silently hidden; and validate the machine-scoped host model with multiple worktree-scoped engines sharing one local runtime store for the same repo. Retire or clearly demote old reconstructive snapshot surfaces so the old architecture cannot drift back in unnoticed.

acceptance.label: The new runtime meets the agreed interactivity, diagnostics-latency, and memory budgets, surfaces unsettled domains explicitly, and keeps runtime status and dashboard surfaces on bounded materialized read models instead of request-time reconstruction.

node.title: Separate mutable engine state from published immutable generations
node.summary: Make published Prism generations an immutable read surface instead of the mutable working substrate. The engine should retain long-lived mutable state internally, publish structurally shared immutable generations for readers, retain only a bounded generation window, and attach explicit per-domain freshness and materialization state to every published generation so readers can distinguish file-local currentness, unsettled cross-file facts, pending reanchor work, and older but valid checkpoints.

acceptance.label: Queries and diagnostics read published generations or bounded materialized derivatives, working mutations no longer rebuild from published Prism state, and retained generation count and memory shape are explicitly bounded.

node.title: Implement the fast-path commit and layered freshness semantics
node.summary: Make small edits publish a new generation after a short serialized commit and emit a monotonic committed delta batch that feeds both downstream semantic settle work and bounded diagnostics/read-model consumers. Fast-path commits may be conservative, but unsettled downstream domains and lagging diagnostics/materializations must be surfaced explicitly through domain-scoped freshness and materialization metadata.

acceptance.label: Small edits publish a new generation after a short serialized commit, emit a monotonic committed delta batch for downstream consumers, and surface all unsettled semantic or diagnostics domains explicitly through freshness/materialization metadata.

node.title: Implement the fast-path commit and layered freshness semantics
node.summary: Define and implement the fast-path correctness envelope: small edits publish a new generation after a minimal serialized commit, emit a monotonic committed delta batch suitable for replay and downstream consumers, and surface unsettled downstream domains explicitly through layered freshness metadata. The fast path may be conservative, but it must never be silently misleading about unsettled cross-file facts or stale derived domains.

acceptance.label: Small edits publish a new generation after a short serialized commit, emit a monotonic committed delta batch for downstream consumers, and surface all unsettled semantic or diagnostics domains explicitly through freshness/materialization metadata.

node.title: Build an incremental diagnostics and runtime read-model plane
node.summary: Introduce an explicit diagnostics/read-model plane alongside the semantic runtime: bounded, incrementally maintained host-level summaries for runtime status, dashboard summary, queue state, domain lag, materialization coverage, and recent operations. These read models are fed from committed delta batches, engine publication, process lifecycle events, and operation events, and they exist so diagnostics consumers never reconstruct runtime state on demand.

acceptance.label: The host owns bounded materialized read models for runtime status, dashboard summary, and recent operation state that are fed incrementally from engine commits, process lifecycle events, and operation events instead of being rebuilt on request.

node.title: Implement a cheap machine-scoped process and instance ledger for diagnostics
node.summary: Replace request-time process introspection with a cheap machine-scoped process and instance ledger that tracks daemon and bridge lifecycle, instance identity, liveness, and scoped ownership. The common diagnostics path should only need bounded liveness checks against known records, while still remaining correct when multiple MCP daemons share one local runtime store and later when the same semantics are backed by Postgres.

acceptance.label: Runtime process and daemon/bridge status comes from an instance-aware process ledger with bounded liveness checks, not broad request-time ps scans, while staying correct for one or many local MCP daemons sharing the same runtime store.

node.title: Route runtimeStatus, dashboard summary, and recent operation views to materialized read models
node.summary: Move runtimeStatus, dashboard summary, and recent operation views onto bounded materialized read models backed by the diagnostics plane and committed delta stream. The common request path must not shell out to broad process scans, walk the workspace, rebuild coordination overlays or plan graphs, or reparse call-log files from disk just to answer status and dashboard queries; instead it should read lag-aware materialized views and expose any unsettled diagnostics domains explicitly.

acceptance.label: Common diagnostics surfaces no longer perform request-time workspace walks, plan graph reconstruction, query-log file reparsing, or broad process scans; they read bounded materialized views that expose explicit domain-scoped freshness and lag state.

node.title: Build an incremental diagnostics and runtime read-model plane
node.summary: Introduce an explicit diagnostics/read-model plane alongside the semantic runtime: bounded, incrementally maintained host-level summaries for runtime status, dashboard summary, queue state, domain lag, materialization coverage, and recent operations. These read models are fed from committed delta batches, engine publication, process lifecycle events, and operation events, and they exist so diagnostics consumers never reconstruct runtime state on demand.

acceptance.label: The host owns bounded materialized read models for runtime status, dashboard summary, and recent operation state that are fed incrementally from engine commits, process lifecycle events, and operation events instead of being rebuilt on request.
